seoTitle: Open Multiple TradingView Charts Free with Vivaldi
metaDescription: See how to view multiple TradingView charts in one window for free using Vivaldi browser's split-screen tabs instead of a paid plan upgrade.
intro: A multi-chart setup is normally locked behind TradingView's paid plans, but the free Vivaldi browser offers a workaround using its built-in split-screen tabs. This guide shows how to view several TradingView charts side by side in one window, covering stocks, crypto, or forex pairs, all without upgrading your account. It's built for day traders, swing traders, and crypto investors who want to monitor multiple markets at once.

steps.heading: Download and install Vivaldi
steps.body: Get the Vivaldi browser and install it, since its split-screen tab feature is what makes the multi-chart layout possible without a TradingView upgrade.

steps.heading: Open TradingView in multiple tabs
steps.body: Load TradingView in separate tabs within Vivaldi, one for each chart or market you want to track.

steps.heading: Arrange tabs into a split-screen layout
steps.body: Use Vivaldi's tab tiling feature to place your TradingView tabs side by side in a single window.

steps.heading: Set up each chart for real-time tracking
steps.body: Select the symbol, timeframe, and indicators you want on each individual chart within the split view.

steps.heading: Save your layout for future sessions
steps.body: Keep the tab arrangement so you can reopen the same multi-chart setup without rebuilding it each time.

faq.question: Is this multi-chart method really free?
faq.answer: Yes, it relies on Vivaldi's free split-screen tab feature rather than any paid TradingView upgrade.

faq.question: Does this work for crypto, forex, and stocks at the same time?
faq.answer: Yes, since each tab loads TradingView independently, you can mix asset types across your split-screen layout.

faq.question: Will this slow down my computer with several charts open?
faq.answer: Running multiple live charts can use more system resources, so performance depends on your computer's specs and how many charts you keep open.

faq.question: Can I use this method on Chrome or another browser?
faq.answer: The split-screen tiling shown here is a Vivaldi-specific feature, so other browsers without similar tab-splitting tools won't replicate it the same way.
